title: ACP session_map grows unbounded; messages shared across sessions
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
decoupling
## Location
src/acp.rs:125, 565-634
## Violation
AcpServer holds `session_map: HashMap<String, String>` for ACP-id to openclaudia-id, inserts on every session/new or fall-through session/load:

```
self.session_map.insert(acp_session_id.clone(), oc_session_id);
self.messages.clear();
```

No eviction path. `self.messages` is a SINGLE shared Vec<Value> cleared on every new/load — the server is effectively single-session despite the map. Two concurrent ACP sessions lose each other's messages with no error.
## Mandated refactor
Pick one model. Recommended: introduce AcpSessionState struct (messages, cancel_flag, oc_session_id, last_activity) keyed by ACP id. Add session/close handling or LRU eviction at N=64. Update handle_session_prompt to look up the session by id.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
